name: Café Buffi (Buchheim Museum)
query: Buchheim Museum, Am Hirschgarten 1, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Homemade cakes and a terrace inside the lakeside art park, with the Höhenried deer to roam to afterward.

name: Hotel Seeblick
query: Hotel Seeblick, Tutzinger Straße 9, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: The best-reviewed restaurant in the village — hearty Bavarian food and a big chestnut beer garden with room for kids.

name: Landgasthof Drei Rosen
query: Landgasthof Drei Rosen, Dorfstraße 11, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Beloved 17th-century village inn with home cooking and a casual garden (closed Mon & Wed).

name: Nordbad Seerestaurant
blurb: Open-air dining literally on the water, with a sandpit to keep the little ones busy.

name: Forsthaus Ilkahöhe
blurb: Hilltop Augustiner restaurant with a panorama to the Zugspitze and open meadow to run around.

name: Marina Seerestaurant
query: Marina Bernried, Am Yachthafen, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Kids watch the boats from a shaded terrace at the yacht harbor (the upscale option).

name: Seerestaurant Undosa
blurb: Lakefront dining with its own beach and a large playground right beside the tables.

name: Kloster Andechs Bräustüberl
blurb: The famous monastery beer garden: monk-brewed beer, a shaded natural playground, and Alpine views — a regional must-do.

name: Midgardhaus (Augustiner am See)
blurb: Classic chestnut-shaded biergarten on the shore where you can bring your own food — great for picky eaters.

name: Würmseestüberl
blurb: Insider tip right on the shore with a bathing meadow, dock and grilled fish.

name: Buchscharner Seewirt
blurb: East-shore beer garden with a playground and bathing area positioned to keep kids in sight; sunset views.

name: Bali Beach
query: Bali Beach, Seepromenade, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Casual summer beach bar on the promenade with volleyball and a kickabout for the kids.

name: Netto Marken-Discount
query: Netto, Am Neuland 1a, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: The only proper grocery in the village, walkable from home.

name: Bäckerei Florian Ziegler
query: Bäckerei Ziegler, Dorfstraße 2, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Traditional village bakery since 1878 — open Sunday morning for fresh rolls.

name: Bernrieder Hofladen (Schlossgut)
query: Bernrieder Hofladen, Tutzinger Straße 12, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Lakeside farm shop with cheese, eggs, smoked fish and coffee, cake and ice cream — a little outing in itself.

name: EDEKA Tutzing (+ Aldi)
blurb: Your closest full-size supermarket, with an Aldi next door for the main shop.

name: EDEKA Seeshaupt
blurb: The main store for the quieter south end of the lake.

name: Wochenmarkt Starnberg
blurb: The biggest regional weekly market — Thursday and Saturday mornings on the Kirchplatz.

name: Wochenmarkt Tutzing
blurb: The closest weekly market, Saturday mornings by the Rathaus.

name: Spielplatz Brahmspromenade
blurb: Playground directly on the lakeshore with Alpine views — the closest great lake-and-play combo to home.

name: Spielplatz am See (Kurpark)
query: Kurpark Spielplatz, Madeleine-Ruoff-Straße, Herrsching am Ammersee
blurb: The region's best lakeside playground: zip line, climbing, carousel, fenced, on a wading beach (Ammersee).

name: Spielplatz Pointstraße
blurb: Fenced neighborhood playground (ages 0–12) — the everyday in-town option.

name: Spielplatz Seepromenade
blurb: Right on the lakefront promenade, easy to pair with a stroll and an ice cream.

name: Bernrieder Park (Buchet)
query: Bernrieder Park, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: On your doorstep: 1.7 km of wild lakefront, ancient beech woods, flat stroller trails, a nature-discovery loop and sandy swim coves.

name: Höhenrieder Park / Schloss Höhenried
query: Schlosspark Höhenried, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: The biggest kid magnet near you: an enclosure of rare white deer kids can feed, plus lake paths and Alpine views.

name: Ilkahöhe
blurb: A 728 m panorama hill (lake + Zugspitze) with a beer garden and a play meadow, walkable or bikeable from Tutzing.

name: Maisinger See & Schlucht
blurb: One of Bavaria's oldest reserves — flat stroller-friendly gorge trails, birds and safe picnic spots.

name: Gut Aiderbichl Iffeldorf
blurb: The near-home pick (~20 min): about 300 rescued tame animals to pet and feed, barrier-free, open every day.

name: Tierpark Hellabrunn
blurb: Munich's great zoo day: two petting areas, two playgrounds including water-play, and handcart rental for toddlers.

name: Wildpark Poing
blurb: Tame deer eat from kids' hands, a big playground and a birds-of-prey show — full-day magic east of Munich.

name: Heiß-Hof
blurb: Hands-on farm fun: a milk 'tankstelle' kids bottle themselves and a 23-flavor ice-cream automat.

name: Starnberger Eiswerkstatt
blurb: Handmade artisan ice cream and fresh waffles — widely called the district's best.

name: GelatóK by Júnior
blurb: Authentic family-run Italian gelato (4.8★) with a sunny, safe outdoor area.

name: Allora Signora – natürlich.eis
blurb: Around 30 natural homemade flavors and a lake-view terrace, two minutes from the boat dock.

name: Da Noi Trattoria & Pizzeria
blurb: The closest genuinely good pizzeria (~9 km), Naples-style and relaxed for families.

name: Trattoria Garibaldi
blurb: Explicitly family-friendly — a garden for the kids, crispy pizza and takeaway.

name: Mille Lire
blurb: Central, wood-fired, big portions, with a takeaway mini-menu.

name: Strandbad Hubl
query: Strandbad Hubl, An der Mühle 1, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Your home beach behind the monastery: lawn, kiosk, a small playground and on-site boat rental and fishing permits (the Hubl family).

name: Strandbad Tutzing Süd
blurb: Shallow shoreline ideal for toddlers, with a big lawn, piers, kiosk and a playground.

name: Strandbad Seeshaupt
blurb: Very shallow, gradual entry — a paradise for small children — with a lawn, playground and boat rental.

name: Familienbad PiORAMA
blurb: Modern indoor family pool with a slide and a jumping facility — the wet-weather and winter default (~25 min).

name: Wasserpark Starnberg
blurb: All-in-one indoor pool with slides and a learner pool, plus an outdoor Strandbad.

name: Fischereikarten Hubl
query: An der Mühle 1, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Buy your Starnberger See fishing permit right in the village (day cards, boat cards) — kids ride along free.

name: Bayerische Seenschifffahrt — Steg Bernried
query: Schiffssteg Bernried, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: The 'white-blue fleet' lake steamers stop right in the village (Easter–mid-Oct); a combo ticket covers the boats and the Buchheim Museum.

name: Bootsverleih Hubl
query: Bootsverleih Hubl, An der Mühle 1, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Rowboats and pedal boats right at the village beach, mid-May to mid-September — just turn up.

name: Segelschule Tutzing
blurb: The closest serious sailing school, with dedicated supervised kids' courses in small groups.

name: Segelschule Starnberg
blurb: Well-established school with courses and private lessons at the busy north end.

name: Hofgut Bernried (dairy)
query: Hofgut Bernried, Tutzinger Straße, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: A real working dairy (~90 cows) right behind the lakeside farm shop — see the cows, buy the cheese.

name: Lothhof (Demeter)
blurb: East-shore Demeter farm with an open barn of cows and calves, free-range chickens, a Hofladen and kids' farm tours.

name: Bernried → Seeshaupt (south shore)
blurb: Short, flat and very doable: past Bernrieder Park to Seeshaupt for a swim and an ice cream (~5–6 km each way).

name: Bernried → Osterseen (Iffeldorf)
blurb: A perfect family ride to the crystal-clear Osterseen bathing lakes; shortenable as needed.

name: East shore: St. Heinrich–Ammerland
blurb: The genuinely family-friendly stretch of the lake loop — mostly paved and low-traffic, past bathing areas.

name: Buchheim Museum der Phantasie
query: Buchheim Museum, Am Hirschgarten 1, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Your headline landmark: a ship-shaped building jutting over the lake in a magical park, with a hands-on kids' atelier (under-6 free).

name: Marcello FUN-ARENA
blurb: A ~2,000 m² indoor playground with a toddler zone — the rainy-day default.

name: Hochseilgarten Ammersee (Wilde Gretel)
query: Hochseilgarten Ammersee, Fahrmannsbachstraße 2, Utting am Ammersee
blurb: A pirate-ship high-ropes park with a Bambini course from age 2.

name: Minigolf Tutzing
blurb: Lakeside minigolf on the waterfront promenade.

name: Gemeindebücherei Tutzing
blurb: A large modern library with a kids' section and picture-book cinema.

name: Deutsches Museum — Kinderreich
blurb: A ~1,200 m² hands-on zone built only for ages 3–8 — the best young-kids indoor experience in Munich.

name: Englischer Garten
blurb: Free all day: meadows, paddling streams, a historic carousel, rowboats and a playground by the Seehaus.

name: Olympiapark (+ SEA LIFE)
blurb: Top playgrounds, summer boats and a toddler area, with an aquarium as a weatherproof backup.

name: Walchensee + Herzogstandbahn
blurb: The most versatile Alpine day: a turquoise swimming lake plus an easy cable car to a café-with-a-view summit (~45–60 min).

name: Blomberg (Blombergbahn)
query: Blombergbahn, Am Blomberg 1, Wackersberg, Bad Tölz
blurb: Best thrill day for the littlest: a summer toboggan run (kids ride from age 3 on a parent's lap) and an adventure park (~40–55 min).

name: Partnachklamm Gorge
blurb: A dramatic, flat gorge walk of waterfalls, tunnels and spray — bring a carrier, not a stroller (~1 hr).

name: Zugspitze + Eibsee
blurb: Germany's highest peak with summer snow to play in, above the turquoise Eibsee — save it for a clear day (~1 hr 10).

name: Munich International School (MIS)
query: Munich International School, Schloss Buchhof, Starnberg
blurb: English-medium IB school, ages 4–18 — the premier expat option (~25 min).

name: CreateSchools
blurb: Bilingual English/German from Year 1 (Cambridge + Bavarian curriculum) — the closest English/bilingual school (~12 min), starts at Grade 1.

name: Grundschule Bernried
query: Grundschule Bernried, Reitweg 13a, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: The local German public primary school — on your own street, a ~2 min walk.

name: Gymnasium Tutzing
blurb: The nearest German state Gymnasium (academic secondary).

name: Montessori Biberkor
blurb: Montessori from Kinderhaus to Abitur — note it's German-medium, not bilingual (~35–40 min).

name: Integratives Kinderhaus Bernried
query: Kinderhaus, Reitweg 13, Bernried am Starnberger See
blurb: Kindergarten (3–6) plus after-school Hort, right next to the house.

name: Krippe am Kloster
blurb: Infant Krippe (ages ~1–3) on the monastery grounds.

name: Waldkinder Bernried
blurb: Parent-run forest kindergarten (ages 3–6).

name: Kinderhaus Seeshaupt
blurb: Municipal Krippe + Kindergarten + Hort (ages 1 to grade 4).

name: Klinikum Starnberg
blurb: The nearest hospital with a real children's clinic — 24/7 pediatric ER and neonatology (~25–30 min).

name: Krankenhaus Weilheim
query: Krankenhaus Weilheim, Johann-Baur-Straße 4, Weilheim in Oberbayern
blurb: Tied-closest ER (~18–22 min); treats kids' injuries but has no pediatric ward.

name: Klinikum Penzberg
blurb: One of the two closest ERs (~20–25 min); no pediatric ward.

name: Dr. von Haunersches Kinderspital
query: Dr. von Haunersches Kinderspital, Lindwurmstraße 4, München
blurb: Major LMU children's hospital and pediatric ER for serious or specialist cases (~45–60 min).

name: Kinderärzte Praxis Seeshaupt
blurb: Closest pediatrician by distance (branch office — confirm clinic days), ~8–12 min.

name: Dr. Birgit Indlekofer (Kinderärztin)
blurb: Full-time standalone pediatric practice (~12–15 min).

name: St. Michaels-Apotheke
blurb: The single closest pharmacy (~7–9 min).

name: Schloss-Apotheke Tutzing
blurb: Pharmacy with its own compounding lab (~12–14 min).

name: Tierarztpraxis Dr. Droste
blurb: In-town vet (small animals + horses) — the first-choice local, ~3 min.

name: Tierklinik Starnberg
blurb: Full clinic with 24/7 emergency — the nearest true emergency vet (~22 min).
